title: Module Manifest Components
goal: Align generic module manifests with MCP-style resource, tool, prompt, schema, and root component classes without adding an MCP runtime dependency.
non_goals: Do not build an MCP server in this slice.
Do not expose source-checkout-only maintainer or dogfooding helpers as shipped host-repo components.
Do not broaden into all schema-inventory or planning-state-maturity issues.

intent.outcome: module manifests can declare adapter-ready components generically
intent.constraints: core package remains MCP/FastMCP dependency-free; contracts stay schema-backed
intent.latitude: field names and exact component payload shape may be chosen locally if they preserve the issue acceptance signals
intent.escalation: escalate if satisfying #510 would require building runtime adapter code or changing package dependency policy

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue with the remaining module-boundary child work.
execution.proof: contract schema checks, full workspace CLI tests, root CLI authority query, ruff, proof selection, and planning doctor

invariants: planning and memory are first-party modules using the same component model
manifest is adapter-ready but MCP runtime-independent

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #485 compresses the root package around generic module contracts; #510 makes module manifests future-adapter-ready.

required_continuation.owner surface: GitHub issue #512 and .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #510 foundation lands; continue with #512 root-surface mapping before closing the parent #485 lane

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: module manifests can describe resources, tools, prompts, schemas, roots, safety, and adapter mapping from one canonical contract
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: actual MCP server/package generation and broader structured-file inventory
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #511 or #512 if their detailed acceptance remains open

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: continue open issue execution lane by lane using package dogfooding
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: promote and implement #510 as the next priority-3 module-boundary milestone
intent_interpretation.review guidance: review that the manifest model is generic and dependency-free rather than MCP-specific runtime work

execution_bounds.allowed paths: module registry contract/schema, module CLI exposure/tests/docs, active planning records
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: broad CLI extraction, new package dependencies, or new top-level command
execution_bounds.stop before touching: package install bootstrap payloads unless generated output is required by tests

stop_conditions.stop when: component model foundation lands and proof passes, or schema/runtime dependency boundary becomes ambiguous
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: if issue requires an MCP implementation, TypeScript generation, or Docker changes
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: if root CLI generation roadmap or planning maturity work becomes necessary
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: if contract checks reveal existing registry shape is incompatible with the proposed component model

context_budget.live working set: #510-#514 issue bodies, module registry contract/schema, module CLI tests
context_budget.externalize before shift: keep this execplan updated with validation and continuation state
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: not needed unless proof routes to memory-specific behavior
context_budget.context-shift triggers: before closing #510 or any child issue

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: complete the next issue lane while dogfooding planning surfaces
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: no MCP runtime dependency; preserve generic module boundaries; commit and push milestone
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: schema field names, compact docs wording, and focused tests
delegated_judgment.escalate when: a future adapter implementation or dependency decision is required

active_milestone.scope: schema-backed component declarations in module registry plus CLI/test/docs exposure
active_milestone.ready: issue detail ingested and existing module registry/test surfaces identified

immediate_next_action: Inspect module registry/schema and module CLI tests, then add the component model contract.
invariants: No MCP or FastMCP runtime dependency.
Adapter mapping is contract metadata, not a new server.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Use resources/tools/prompts/schemas/roots as the generic module component vocabulary.
Declare safety metadata on tool components.
open_questions_to_close: Does this slice fully satisfy #511-#514 or only #510 foundation?
validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Module registry schema accepts explicit component classes.
Current root/planning/memory module surfaces are mapped as components.
Tool components carry safety and structured-result metadata.
Future MCP adapter boundary is visible without adding runtime dependencies.

execution_run.what happened: Added schema-backed module component model metadata to module_registry.json, exposed it through the modules command, covered it in module CLI tests, and recorded the contract in contract_inventory.json.
execution_run.scope touched: module registry contract/schema, modules command rendering, focused tests, README, planning closeout surfaces
execution_run.validations run: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed <paths> --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: #510 foundation is complete; #512 remains open for root-surface mapping and #485 remains the broader parent lane.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan, close satisfied issues, refresh external evidence, then continue with the next open issue.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no MCP server, runtime dependency, or new command was added.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes for #510 foundation and child slices #511, #513, and #514; #512 remains partially open because root workspace surfaces were not fully modeled as components.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used summary, report, skills, proof, doctor, reconcile, and archive helper surfaces.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; the manifest is adapter-ready metadata rather than MCP runtime implementation.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
proof_report.proof achieved now: validation and closure checks passed for the bounded slice.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Contract tooling reported no drift, full workspace CLI tests passed, ruff passed, root CLI authority classified the CLI edit as allowed derived/runtime work, and planning doctor reported healthy.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #510 align generic module manifests with MCP-style resources, tools, prompts, schemas, and structured results
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Module registry now schema-validates resources, tools, prompts, schemas, roots, tool safety metadata, and adapter boundary; tests and contract checks pass.
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane module-boundary-compression (#512 remains open for root-surface mapping)

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep the package streamlined, modular, and small while preserving leverage; canonical authority belongs in contracts/config/planning/memory/checks rather than prose adapters.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: contract metadata over new runtime surface
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make future adapters cheaper without making ordinary package entry heavier?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The #510 foundation is implemented, while the broader module-boundary lane remains open for child-specific root-surface mapping and later compression work.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Contract tooling passed; tests/test_workspace_cli.py passed; ruff passed; root_cli_authority and planning doctor passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen if module components cannot project to a future adapter without adding core MCP runtime dependency, or if #512 finds missing root mapping that invalidates the component contract.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Generic module manifests now carry schema-backed component declarations and an MCP-style future adapter boundary without adding a runtime dependency.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: contract tooling, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, root CLI authority, proof selection, planning doctor, and reconcile passed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): contract_inventory.json and README were updated; no memory capture needed.
execution_summary.resume from: Refresh external evidence after closing satisfied issues, then continue with #512.

discard.summary: Command output and validation detail stay in git history and archived proof.

continuation.summary: Continue with #512 and the module-boundary-compression roadmap lane.

memory.summary: contract_inventory.json and README were updated; no memory capture needed.

docs.summary: README now notes module manifests declare adapter-ready components without an MCP runtime dependency.

issue_follow_up.summary: #512 remains open for root workspace surface mapping.

drift_log: 2026-04-28: Promoted #510 from module-boundary-compression into active planning after refreshing open external issues and package summary.
